cards.title: Market-Linked Products
cards.content:
• Traditional vs Non-Traditional Products
• Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs)
• Net Asset Value (NAV) Calculation
• Universal Life Insurance Features

cards.title: 1. Why Non-Traditional?
cards.content: Traditional products are 'bundled' (opaque). Non-traditional products bring Transparency through 'Unbundling'—separating risk cover, expenses, and investment.

cards.title: 2. ULIP Core Concepts
cards.content:
Premiums buy 'Units' in a fund (Equity, Debt, etc.). 
NAV: The price per unit.
Risk: Investment risk is 100% borne by the POLICYHOLDER.
Lock-in: Mandatory 5 years.

cards.title: 3. Flexibility in ULIPs
cards.content:
Fund Switching: Move money between Equity & Debt.
Premium Holiday: Temporary stop in payments if fund value is high enough.

cards.title: 4. Key Features
cards.content: Highly flexible. Allows policyholders to change the premium amount and Sum Assured based on their financial life changes.

sections.title: 🚀 Exam Pro-Tips (Repeated Themes)
items: ULIP Investment Risk: Borne by the Policyholder.
Unbundling: Separating protection, savings, and expenses.
ULIP Value: Depends on the NAV of the units.
Switching option: Only available in ULIPs.

cards.title: Q1. Who bears the investment risk in a ULIP?
